# Transcrição: 5. Reactivating Sold Customers

- **Fonte:** 5. Reactivating Sold Customers
- **Duração:** 03:44
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

## [00:00 - 03:00]

Okay, what we're going to do here is reactivate sold customers. That's past customers that you sold and haven't been in contact with. Those of you who have been successfully selling for some time know exactly who I'm talking about. Just think about the list in your head. Regardless of how long it has been since you spoke to John that you sold nine months ago, no matter how long it's been, okay, we're going to show you how to reactivate with a call, follow-up letter, email, possibly a text, video, and then a personal visit. Okay? If you can't call the people that have paid you, folks, look, you're in trouble. If you can't call the people that paid you in the past and put your kids through school, put food on your dinner table, I assure you, you're never going to fully maximize the opportunities in the marketplace and the economy will always be difficult for you. You're making life hard by not following people up. John, I bought for you before. Let's let you know the John that bought from you before, let's take good care of that John, okay? Look, I want to make 10 of these calls each day minimum. I'm going to make 10 calls each day, even today, to people that bought from me six months, nine months, 12 months, two years ago. They bought from me three months ago. I just need 10 people every day. John, just want to check with you how you're doing, what's happened. The purpose of this call is not to get out of obscurity. He already knows who I am. The purpose now, you remember two problems, obscurity, two, I'm not thinking about you when I go to do something next time. I know you, but I'm not thinking about you, okay? So the purpose of this call is number one, to remind him you're there, okay? And then number two, to serve that client. He bought from you, serve him now, take care of him. Do what you said you were going to do when you made the sale, man. Number three, I want to update all information, emails, text number, Facebook, Twitter, anything and everything, where you live and what's happening, what are you doing, anything you can get this hard data. One of the biggest mistakes I've made in my career is I didn't capture all this data and keep it and keep it updated. Number four, find out where John is in the cycle if you can find that out here. Number five, we want to set up a personal visit. That's right, I'm going to go see him. And number six, I want to get referrals if possible. He'll give them to you if he asks the right way. We'll cover that in another module. Look any problems that come up as part of the call, what are you going to do? Handle them as opportunities, right? I'll even ask for problems, any problems. Anything you'd want different, anything I can do for you at this time, anything I can do to support you, anything I can do to make the experience better. I know salespeople who are terrified of this question. Why, man? Don't be scared of a problem. Embrace and love the problems. You want the problems. If there's a problem, hey, become Superman and solve it. Ultimately, what I want to do here is get on a schedule of calling my sole customers back and I want to have that like nine to 10 time. I'm calling all my sole customers. I'm doing 10 of them and I'm going to do 10 to 15 minutes. I'm not going to take three hours to do these 10 calls.

## [03:00 - 03:44]

You crazy? Okay. Before every call is over and you talk to somebody, you didn't just leave a message. Before every one of these calls is over, tell the client. Then the next time is that you'll be contacting him. Okay? Hey, I'm going to call you back in a couple of weeks and see what we can set up for lunch. See what I'm going to set up a second call. Or, hey, give me about two months and we'll get back with you, let you know what's happening with that. Right? Always commit to another call. Get all the information you possibly can out of this call. You want data. You know, knowledge is power. Text, email, address. Who knows? Maybe he moved his offices. Maybe he moved his home. That's a great call. Next module, I'll give you a sample call from me.
